title: ranunculus reptans var. samolifolius
intro: Ranunculus reptans var. samolifolius, also known as Water Crowfoot, is a type of aquatic or semi-aquatic plant belonging to the Ranunculaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe wounds and reduce swelling. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as supporting the immune system and reducing fever, and has been used to treat conditions like skin irritations and respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in infusions, decoctions, and salves, and its leaves can be dried and used as a tea or added to creams and ointments.
